title: Business Reasoning Canvas layout direction
summary: Choose whether a journey is always the reading spine or optional for proposition-shaped reasoning.

sections.title: Primary skeleton · Journey spine
sections.summary: Actors and outcomes lead the reading order; assumptions, economics, risks, and experiments attach to the relevant stage.

items.title: Discover
points: Assumption · the problem is frequent enough to matter.
Risk · current workaround may already be sufficient.

items.title: Evaluate
items.detail: Actor compares the proposition with the current alternative.
points: Economics · value must exceed switching cost.
Experiment · concierge trial measures willingness to change.

items.title: Adopt
items.detail: Actor reaches the promised outcome and generates learning.
points: Evidence · retention tests whether value repeats.

sections.title: Choose the Business Reasoning Canvas skeleton
sections.summary: Both retain actors, outcomes, assumptions, economics, risks, and experiments.

options.label: Journey spine
options.detail: Use the actor journey as the stable reading order and attach business reasoning to its stages.
points: 9/10 · Strong narrative causality from actor to outcome.
Can force an artificial sequence onto proposition-shaped work.

options.label: Proposition blocks · spine optional
options.detail: Lead with actor, outcome, proposition, economics, and risks; add a journey spine only when sequence carries meaning.
points: 8/10 · Better for early propositions and non-linear business models.
Needs another cue to preserve narrative reading order.
